metrics.narrative: Pain and consolidation value are qualitatively well established (homegrown supplier-risk/contract-renewal tools, manual invoice-to-PO/contract matching gaps, non-catalog spend visibility gaps) from the three Jun 2025 demos, but no buyer-quantified ROI figure or savings target has been captured; the only dollar figures on record are illustrative (a $1M vs realistic $5M service-contract budgeting example, demo#2, 26 Jun 2025) rather than a buyer business case.

champion.narrative: Gaurav (Rakesh Desai) has driven essentially every step since late 2025 — coordinating the Jan 2026 onsite, offering the ANA POC (25 Feb 2026), connecting internally on leadership next steps (26 May 2026) — which is real champion behavior. But he has gone silent on the one question that matters most: the SAP Ariba beta outcome, with 'no update' logged on 16 Jun 2026 despite follow-ups on 8, 16, and 29 Jun 2026. This is a champion at real risk of having lost internal traction or momentum.

competition.narrative: SAP Ariba is the dominant, active threat: Allstate confirmed on 15 Dec 2025 it was running a formal 'SAP vs Zycus' comparison and has been in an Ariba workshop/beta program since at least 26 May 2026, which is the explicit gate on the decision. Coupa and Ivalua remain on the Competitors field but show no active-evaluation evidence in any call. SAP Fieldglass (with Beeline as an alternative) is a separate, VMS-only competitive thread where Zycus explicitly is not trying to displace the incumbent (15 Jul 2025 VMS demo).

identify_pain.narrative: Pain is deeply documented across the three Jun 2025 demos: manual contract redlining in Word with legal check-in/out, homegrown systems for supplier risk and contract-renewal tracking, non-PO invoices (especially tax payments) that don't tie to purchase orders despite a $20,000 compliance threshold, and only 70% of demand planned centrally through procurement (demo#1-3, 23-26 Jun 2025). The 15 Jul 2025 VMS session added a related but distinct pain: manual SharePoint timesheet capture outside Fieldglass.

paper_process.narrative: No MSA/SOW/commercial paper track has opened; the deal is still pre-decision. The one real forcing function on record is the incumbent Ariba agreement, which Allstate noted on 21 Jan 2026 re-signs with roughly 12 months remaining — meaning there is no near-term contractual cliff pressuring Allstate to decide quickly.

economic_buyer.narrative: Casey McDowell (VP - Global Enterprise Procurement) and Cheryl Harris (SVP, Procurement) are both named Executive Sponsors on the Opportunity Contact Roles, and the deal owner has been trying to secure a direct Chicago exec session with Casey since 4 Feb 2026 ('working on setting up executive session with Casey in Chicago', Next Step 2026-02-04). As of the 29 Jun 2026 Next Step entry, neither has appeared on a recorded call or been confirmed as directly engaged — the economic buyer is identified but not engaged.

decision_process.narrative: Allstate set its own process on 21 Jan 2026 ('decision in next 3/4 months'), implying a decision window of roughly Apr-May 2026; that window has passed with no new date communicated as of the 29 Jun 2026 Next Step. The process involves Gaurav coordinating internally with 'leadership' before Zycus gets a read on next steps (Next Step, 26 May 2026), so Zycus has limited direct visibility into the actual internal decision mechanics.

decision_criteria.narrative: Criteria are well documented: the Jun 2025 demos surfaced explicit RFP-scoring questions ('what kind of scoring process do you follow internally, and how involved is the business... evaluating these RFP', demo#1, 23 Jun 2025), and the 21 Jan 2026 onsite focused the evaluation on roadmap, product enhancements, support, and a detailed SAP-to-Zycus implementation timeline (two-phase, ~9-month lift-and-shift).

items.detail: Close date 2026-07-31 is 25 days out, but the SAP Ariba beta has not concluded, the POC has not started, reference calls are not scheduled, and commercials/legal/InfoSec have not begun. A 9-month implementation timeline was discussed (January 2026 on-site), making the July close date impossible. The deal will slip unless Allstate accelerates post-beta or accepts a later go-live.

SAP Ariba beta is the gate blocking the decision. Gaurav has not shared the beta outcome despite follow-ups June 8 and June 16, 2026. If SAP's beta demonstrates comparable AI capabilities, improved roadmap, or lower switching cost, Allstate may choose to renew Ariba rather than migrate to the relevant stakeholder competitive threat is HIGH and unresolved.

Casey McDowell (VP Procurement, Executive Sponsor) and Cheryl Harris (SVP Procurement, Executive Sponsor) are mapped but have never appeared on a call or confirmed direct engagement. The executive session planned February-April 2026 per Next Step was not confirmed as completed. Passive executive sponsorship is a risk—without Casey/Cheryl buy-in, the deal can lose to SAP incumbent inertia or stall indefinitely.

Reference calls were requested January 2026 ('need to set up different references ASAP' per Next Step 2026-01-21) and committed on the January on-site (5 calls, 2 with AI-focused customers), but none have been scheduled as of June 2026. Allstate is awaiting peer proof that Zycus can migrate them off SAP and that AI adoption is real. Without references, the decision risk stays high and favors the SAP incumbent.

ANA (autonomous negotiation agent) POC was offered February 2026 and has not started. The POC is the primary vehicle to prove Zycus AI is production-ready vs SAP's beta-stage capabilities. Without executing it, Zycus's AI differentiation remains a roadmap promise, not a proven advantage.

confidence_signals.summary: Deep historical qualification (multiple 2025 demos, a January 2026 onsite) is offset by roughly five and a half months of thin, one-sided rep outreach since, with only a single confirmed buyer email reply on 22 May 2026.

north_star_verdict.math: Created 6 Mar 2024; formal SAP-vs-Zycus bake-off confirmed 15 Dec 2025; Shortlisted since the Jan 2026 onsite. Today is 7 Jul 2026, 24 days from the 31 Jul 2026 close date. Last genuine two-way buyer reply was 22 May 2026 (46 days ago, Gaurav: 'let me connect with our leadership'); three rep follow-ups since (8 Jun, 16 Jun, 29 Jun 2026) drew no reply. No POC, reference calls, refreshed commercials, or contracting work have started, so the current close date cannot be met.
evidence: Next Step 2026-05-21 (Gaurav email): 'Our Technology team is still planning for resources allocation who can dedicate their time in Zycus POC as there are still some activities happening around SAP Ariba workshop/beta program.'
Next Step 2026-05-22 (Gaurav email): 'Let me connect with our leadership and take their inputs on how to proceed. Give me some time and I will get back to you.' — no reply since.
Next Step 2026-06-16: 'no update on the beta or evaluation from gaurav'
Next Step 2026-01-21 (Touchpoint 5): decision expected 'in next 3/4 months' (i.e. by ~April/May 2026) — that window has passed with no resolution.
north_star_verdict.headline: Stalled at Shortlisted: Allstate has gone quiet since 22 May 2026 (46 days ago) while running a parallel SAP Ariba beta, and the ANA POC plus AI references promised since Feb 2026 remain undelivered, leaving the 31 Jul 2026 close date unreachable on current pace.
north_star_verdict.recommended_forecast: Pipeline (not Upside Key Deal) until the SAP Ariba beta outcome is known and the POC/reference/commercial gates clear; realistic close is Q4 2026 or later given the undiscussed 9-month implementation runway.

flags: Break the single-threaded Gaurav bottleneck: re-engage Casey McDowell and Mohit Bothra directly for a firm SAP-beta decision date, since Gaurav has gone quiet since 22 May 2026 despite three follow-ups.
best_practice_check.summary: The deal's biggest lever right now is forcing a resolution on the SAP Ariba beta and converting the still-passive executive sponsors into active air cover before the close date lapses further.

customer_preference.evidence: No buyer-voiced statement of preference for Zycus over SAP Ariba exists in any call or Next Step note; the buyer is described as still 'doing a SAP Ariba workshop/beta program' as recently as 26 May 2026 (Next_Step_History__c).

competitive_position.summary: SAP Ariba is the dominant, active threat and the explicit gate on Allstate's decision via its ongoing workshop/beta program. Coupa and Ivalua remain listed on the account but show no active-evaluation evidence in any call or note. SAP Fieldglass (with Beeline as an alternative) is a separate VMS-only thread where Zycus is positioned as complementary, not competitive. Icertis surfaced once as a passing CLM question over a year ago with no follow-through.

deal_scores_evidence.summary: Allstate is a $1.5M full-S2P shortlist deal with deep historical qualification (three product demos in June 2025, a VMS deep-dive in July 2025, and a 5+ hour executive onsite in January 2026) that has stalled in an SAP Ariba beta stand-off since December 2025. The champion has gone quiet on the beta outcome despite three follow-ups since 22 May 2026, and the 31 Jul 2026 close date has no supporting recent momentum behind it.

deal_risk.text: Forecast (Upside Key Deal) is not supported by current-stage evidence — no POC, no delivered references, no refreshed proposal, and an unresolved incumbent beta, all against a close date only 22 days away.

The champion may be losing internal traction — Gaurav has gone quiet on the beta outcome across three consecutive follow-ups (8, 16, 29 Jun 2026) after historically driving every prior step (onsite planning, POC offer, reference requests).

win_position.text: Deep historical qualification — three product demos (23, 25, 26 Jun 2025), a VMS deep-dive (15 Jul 2025), and a 5+ hour executive onsite (21 Jan 2026) established broad multi-thread engagement and a strong AI/Merlin differentiation story against SAP Ariba.

SAP Ariba's incumbent beta is the explicit gate — Next Step notes from 26 May, 8 Jun, and 16 Jun 2026 confirm Allstate is still running a 'SAP Ariba workshop/beta program' and Gaurav has given 'no update on the beta or evaluation' as of 16 Jun 2026.

Economic buyers are mapped but never engaged — Casey McDowell (VP Global Enterprise Procurement) and Cheryl Harris (SVP Procurement) are named Executive Sponsors, but a Chicago exec session requested since 4 Feb 2026 has never been confirmed as held.

Committed proof points are roughly 170 days overdue — five reference calls (two AI-at-scale) and an ANA POC were promised at the 21 Jan 2026 onsite and re-offered 25 Feb 2026, and neither has started as of the 29 Jun 2026 Next Step.

deal_momentum.text: Rep is chasing into silence since 22 May 2026 — the last confirmed two-way buyer touch was a buyer email reply on 22 May 2026 (roughly 48 days ago); three subsequent follow-ups (8, 16, 29 Jun 2026) show no logged buyer reply.
No meeting logged since the 21 Jan 2026 onsite — roughly five and a half months without a fresh call, workshop, or POC session.
The close date has no forward motion behind it — 22 days from close (31 Jul 2026) with the SAP beta unresolved, no POC started, and no updated commercial proposal issued.

customer_commitment.text: Allstate self-set a decision window at the January onsite ('decision in next 3/4 months', 21 Jan 2026), implying a decision by roughly April-May 2026; that window has passed with no new date communicated.

The incumbent Ariba renewal is not an urgent forcing function — Allstate noted on 21 Jan 2026 that the current Ariba agreement re-signs with about 12 months remaining, removing time pressure to decide.
